rationale: Schema.optionalWith Partial(candidate)-vs-Complete(authoritative) streaming model collides with langextract V1's explicit streaming-deferred lock, so it cannot extend goals/langextract-capability; it seeds the new sibling deterministic-doc-structure-extraction packet alongside the regex extractor. @beep/langextract already has GroundedExtraction schemas to compose against.
cautions: langextract V1 SPEC locks streaming as not-public-API — Partial/Complete gate needs a standards decision, do NOT route as extend-goal
TalentScore is external private project — port the optionalWith partial-vs-final PATTERN, not code

rationale: Repo already owns @effect/sql-style typed-JSONB persistence: packages/workspace/server ThreadStore Drizzle repo plus the EntitySchema.persist.jsonb read-revalidation convention. parseJson schema round-trip is the standing schema-kit pattern. Reference-only.

rationale: Contract-first RPC + OTLP tracing already shipped: packages/agents/use-cases Chat.rpc.ts is the chat RPC contract (chat is on the DUP-SKIP substrate list) and @beep/observability provides OTLP + structured logging. Only the audit wrap-middleware logger is a minor pattern; coordinate it with goals/agent-governance-control-plane rather than rebuilding transport.
cautions: chat/RPC + observability are dup substrate; WebSocket+NDJSON transport is reference-grade only

rationale: Boundary validation returning a structured result instead of throwing is the core schema-kit pattern: S.decodeUnknownEffect at the retrieval->logic wall yields field-path-prefixed typed ParseError. Fully owned by @beep/schema. Reference-only.
cautions: catalog gapStatus=dup — no packet opens

rationale: Interface-segregated tool contexts + adapter DI is already realized as Effect Layer capability splits in the two existing MCP servers (@beep/nlp-mcp, @beep/m365-mcp) with Tools/Handlers separation. Both MCP servers are on the DUP-SKIP substrate list. Reference-only.

rationale: Typed error hierarchy with code + structured payload targets the bare skeleton legal drivers' <Name>.errors.ts modules (courtlistener/ecfr/dol/federal-register currently export only VERSION='0.0.0'), so it belongs to the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen wedge that implements them via the runpod/acp codegen precedent.
cautions: harvest-mcp license flagged unknown — reimplement the Schema.TaggedError taxonomy, do not copy

rationale: Bounded-concurrency semaphore + per-file transaction ingestion is the RAG ingestion workflow the seed routes to rag-retrieval-projection, re-expressed as Effect Stream.mapEffect with bounded parallelism + per-item PGlite/Drizzle txn. EF-27 bounded fan-out is documented but the ingestion projection has no execution home.
cautions: lawyergpt license unknown — reimplement; lawyergpt's un-gated raw-chunk RAG is an anti-pattern beep rejects (outputs must carry CandidateClaim+Evidence)

rationale: HTTP status-code humanization + typed API-error mapping as Schema.TaggedError variants targets exactly the gov drivers (CourtListener/eCFR/Federal-Register/DOL/GovInfo) the gov-legal-data-driver-codegen wedge implements. govinfo is substantial (150 files) and needs the auth/retry/cache client layer; the other four are bare skeletons.
cautions: CourtListener V4 enforces 'Authorization: Token <key>' (DRF, not Bearer); GovInfo/DOL need api.data.gov api_key; eCFR/FedReg keyless

rationale: Failure-vs-defect split is documented as law EF-31 and implemented per-driver in anthropic, but the shared decodeOrDie + isDefect/isRetryable foundation helper has no consolidation home; the seed routes this DRY-extraction to effect-orchestration-patterns.
cautions: EF-31 already documented + per-driver impl exists — scope is library consolidation, not greenfield

rationale: Centralized Schedule retry-policy library (llmRetry/networkRetry/databaseRetry) is the seed's headline net-new for effect-orchestration-patterns: retry policies are currently inline-duplicated per-driver (Schedule.exponential in anthropic Anthropic.service.ts:106 / Anthropic.repair.ts:105, openai-compat) with no shared foundation module.
cautions: first-party Effect code, no licensing concern; reuse + DRY-extract existing per-driver Schedule.exponential, do not rebuild EF-25/26

rationale: Layer.unwrapEffect lazy provider selection has zero occurrences anywhere under packages/**/src (provider wiring is static Layer composition today); the seed routes this net-new effectful provider-selection layer over anthropic|openai-compat|xai|venice-ai to effect-orchestration-patterns, cross-linking the multi-provider-llm-dispatch-fallback split.
cautions: validate Layer.unwrapEffect against the vendored effect-v4 API before adopting
fallback-chain strand overlaps the multi-provider-llm-dispatch-fallback split — coordinate

rationale: Domain error taxonomy with isDefect/isRetryable classification predicates is the same foundation typed-error helper as research-squad#2, to be shared across all slices; consolidates into effect-orchestration-patterns alongside the retry-policy + provider-selection net-new.
cautions: EF-31 + TaggedErrorClass already exist in anthropic — scope is shared-predicate extraction

rationale: Bounded-concurrency subagent fan-out with graceful degradation maps EF-27 (documented) onto concrete @beep/nlp-mcp batch-extraction + agents orchestration via Effect.forEach concurrency + per-item recovery; the orchestration library consolidation is net-new in effect-orchestration-patterns.
cautions: EF-27 bounded fan-out already documented + applied operationally — concretize, don't rebuild the law

rationale: Lazy module-attribute loading of heavy data structures is an Effect construction pattern (lazy/memoized Layer for heavy legal reference-data tables), a reference-grade addition to effect-orchestration-patterns; its concrete consumer is the court-vocabulary-resolver courts-db dataset.
cautions: recommendation=reference, P3 — pattern note, not a build

rationale: Parameter-object pattern with invariant validation for search APIs maps to @beep/uspto + @beep/govinfo Search-request S.Class models (attorney-filter vocabulary, refined bounds). uspto is hand-rolled on ODP and lacks a query-DSL/parameter model; extend it (never restart) under the uspto-patent-driver-depth wedge.
cautions: uspto targets ODP (data.uspto.gov) — never reintroduce PatentsView; searchApplications today only forwards a raw q= string

rationale: Regex entity-and-relationship extraction into a node/link graph is the deterministic doc-structure regex extractor that the seed routes to the new deterministic-doc-structure-extraction sibling (no deterministic legal/contract-structure extractor exists in @beep/nlp); candidates feed @beep/epistemic + the planned FalkorDB projection.
cautions: keep provider-neutral + span-bearing, off the entity-span-lossy AnnotatedDocument envelope; LegalEase license unknown — reimplement

rationale: Triple-stream BM25+vector+graph hybrid retrieval fused via RRF is the GraphRAG retrieval service that is PLANNED-not-built (docs/BEEPGRAPH_ARCHITECTURE.md); it lands in rag-retrieval-projection over @beep/semantic-web + the planned FalkorDB projection + PGlite vectors, coordinating with trustgraph-port to avoid two retrieval layers.
cautions: RRF k=60 (Cormack 2009): fuse on rank only, do not score-normalize across streams
overlaps agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges + trustgraph-port — single retrieval layer

rationale: Scale-driven name-index/edge-key/node-degree side indexes are projection-tier design for the FalkorDB/PGlite BeepGraph projection; this is study-grade index design for the rag-retrieval-projection / trustgraph graph store (zero such index design exists in packages).
cautions: recommendation=study; coordinate the graph-store index design with trustgraph-port (FalkorDB owner)

rationale: Court jurisdiction taxonomy (federal/state/tribal/territory/military) is a SKOS controlled vocabulary — no SKOS exists in @beep/rdf or semantic-web vocab today (only owl/prov/rdfs/rdf/xsd/oa). The seed routes this cluster to the new court-vocabulary-resolver, seeding a SKOS jurisdiction vocab in @beep/rdf + value vocab on law-practice Matter/OfficeAction; ip-law-kg owns only abstract OWL Court/Jurisdiction nodes.
cautions: courtlistener driver is a bare VERSION-only skeleton; keep graph-shape Court/Jurisdiction nodes owned by ip-law-knowledge-graph

rationale: Temporal-validity filtering by date_found generalizes to as-of-date / bitemporal fact scoping over the KG — explicitly DEFERRED by the completed epistemic gate, so it is net-new in agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges (tvalid/tvalidEnd), with the concrete courts-by-date case coordinated to court-vocabulary-resolver.
cautions: bitemporal edge store was deferred from the completed epistemic gate — do not reopen its SPEC; never-overwrite/always-version semantics

rationale: Parent/child court disambiguation (most-specific-wins reduction) is the resolver logic of court-vocabulary-resolver — subsumption-aware match reduction over SKOS broader relations; no court-string resolver exists in packages.
cautions: courts-db is BSD-2 (vendor dataset with attribution); reimplement resolver logic, avoid AGPL CourtListener server code

rationale: Amendment-chain / transitive supersede resolution maps to the deferred amends/supersedes lineage edges + FRBR/LRM version resolution (supersededBy/isLatest) net-new in agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges, over no-fabrication entity resolution for epistemic candidate claims; FalkorDB lineage edges coordinate with trustgraph-port.
cautions: no conflict/supersede edge store exists; epistemic ClaimLifecycle is forward-only today; doc-haus license unknown — reuse semantics, not code

rationale: WIPO-IPC/CPC classification taxonomy (section+class maps, hierarchical lookup) is an IP controlled-vocabulary seed; it adds a deferred SKOS-taxonomy scope note to goals/ip-law-knowledge-graph (the IP ontology owner) + @beep/rdf vocab, sharing the resolver pattern with court-vocabulary-resolver.
cautions: non-invasive dated research note — do not reopen ip-law-knowledge-graph SPEC; optional MCP resource via @beep/nlp-mcp

rationale: Bounded BFS over the patent citation graph (forward/backward/both) is a graph-traversal projection over law-practice PriorArtReference, sourced via @beep/uspto and materialized in the planned FalkorDB citation projection — the graph channel of rag-retrieval-projection, coordinating with trustgraph-port.
cautions: citation data sourced via the hand-rolled uspto ODP driver (extend, not restart); traversal is a projection over rebuildable graph, not authority

rationale: Conversation branching via parent_id + branch_index adds a branchIndex sibling-ordering variant alongside the existing Turn.parentTurnId; goals/workspace-thread-domain already owns Turn branching ('branching = parent-turn lineage'), so this is a small deferred extend to that goal, not a new packet.
cautions: Turn already has parentTurnId (Option) — branchIndex is sibling ordering only; non-invasive scope note on workspace-thread-domain

rationale: Retention scoring (salience x exp temporal decay + reinforcement, hot/warm/cold/evictable tiers) + accessCount/strength is net-new in agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges — no working-memory/decay tier exists in packages; feeds a @beep/agents-use-cases working-set service + epistemic ClaimProjection prioritization.

rationale: Four-tier consolidation taxonomy (working/episodic/semantic/procedural) ConsolidationTier enum has zero presence in packages — net-new in agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges extending CandidateClaim; the four-LAYER taxonomy is already a binding decision in standards/memory-architecture/01.
cautions: per-fact confidence (Evidence/EvidenceSpan UnitInterval) + the semantic tier already exist (dup parts) — only ConsolidationTier enum + working tier are net-new

rationale: Heuristic confidence scoring for typed memory relations (supersedes/contradicts/extends/derives/related) is the net-new ClaimRelation value + confidence/contradiction service feeding ClaimGate — no conflict detection exists; lands in agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges.

rationale: Candidate->approved redline gate adds rejected/superseded ClaimLifecycle STATES (today rejected is only a ClaimGate verdict; ClaimLifecycle is forward-only candidate->shape_valid->consistency_checked->admitted) + a ClaimGate conflict/supersede pass — the deferred epistemic work routes to agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges; the approval-queue/ctx.ask permission strand coordinates with agent-governance-control-plane.

rationale: Retrieval-grounded legal agent prompt + per-agent tool allowlist + handoff-not-workaround boundaries maps to packages/agents/domain Agent/Skill (currently stubs); the agent prompt/skill/tool-allowlist surface is within goals/agentic-professional-runtime scope, with tool-gating coordinated to agent-governance-control-plane.

rationale: Agent-memory compression/snapshot/restore prompt suite is a memory-management capability (new @beep/agents-use-cases MemorySnapshot schema + Thread context-reduction process) belonging to the agent-memory-tiers-bitemporal-edges wedge; no snapshot/restore exists.

rationale: Deterministic weighted scoring with dealbreaker rules over LLM-extracted typed facts (RETRIEVAL/LOGIC split) targets packages/epistemic/use-cases/ClaimGate, which already owns the deterministic gate substrate; per the routing rule ClaimGate/lifecycle items are dup/already-covered, so this attaches as an explainable-scoring rule layer rather than a new packet.
